summary_one_line: Input must supply a well-formed HTTP response whose status-line and headers fully parse so k->header clears to false, exiting header-parse mode.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A response payload containing the exact 'HTTP/' protocol-version literal (bytes 47 48 54 54 50 at offset 0x24) plus a syntactically complete header block terminated by CRLF, so Curl_http_readwrite_headers finishes parsing and clears k->header, letting the false branch at line 630 (and the body-write path at line 666) be taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a stable, concentrated 'HTTP/' literal at offsets 0x24-0x28 (47(G)48(H)54(T)54(T)50(P)) followed by structured header bytes ('me@Lomewhere\r\n...o: fak'), and they actually hit the false side and body-write region (k->header taken-false count 20, line 666 reached, k->bytecount/Curl_client_write rows all [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds replace those structured offsets with '%'-dominated noise (e.g. 0x24 is 50(P)/42(B)/9e/2e, 0x25-0x29 are %-runs), never assembling a parseable HTTP header block, so Curl_http_readwrite_headers keeps k->header set, the false side is never taken (loser taken-false = 0), and execution stays in header-parse mode.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where curl's header parser compares input bytes against the 'HTTP/' protocol token and other header literals, then substitutes the matching operand directly into the input at the comparison offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly havoc-mutate the multi-byte 'HTTP/' literal and header structure, which they fail to do in 10/10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the header-mode test if(k->header) at transfer.c:630 with the false side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     if(k->header) {
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs are I2S-delta pairs where the I2S-equipped fuzzer resolves 10/10 and its non-I2S sibling blocks 10/10.
The winner clears k->header and proceeds to the body-write block at line 666, which the loser never reaches.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
if(k->header) {  <-- BLOCKER
  d=1   L 666  T=17 F=23  T=0 F=0  if(!k->header && (nread > 0 || is_empty_data)) {

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry a stable HTTP/ literal at offset 0x24 that losers do not.
Winner seed bytes show the GHTTP/ literal and a CRLF-terminated header line in the hex dump.
Loser seeds at the same offsets are dominated by '%' noise rather than a parseable header block.
cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes a CMP operand into the input at matching offsets; naive and value_profile lack it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, matching its loser role in pair 2.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 426 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed d998cc3bf9d18a0c`; observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 7 and 8 in the ancestor chain, the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog/vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A naive or value_profile trial resolving the false side of if(k->header) at a rate comparable to cmplog/vpc, or cmplog resolving it even when the input cannot contain the 'HTTP/' literal (e.g. that byte region forced to non-comparison data), would refute the I2S-literal-substitution mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: I infer the substituted CMP operand is specifically the 'HTTP/' protocol token from byte stability at 0x24, but the prompt does not directly name which comparison curl's header parser performs, so the exact operand identity is inferred not proven.
